title: Splunk `_audit` Tamper Indicators — Saved Search Deletes and Role Changes

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
PCI-DSS v3.2.1 10.5 Log integrity — UC detects Splunk _audit tamper indicators (saved-search deletes, role changes) that would compromise log integrity, the direct v3.2.1 10.5 evidence.

spl:
index=_audit (action="delete" OR operation="delete") earliest=-24h
| where match(object,"(?i)savedsearch|alert|correlation|pci|cde)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first, values(user) as actors by object
| sort first
value: Alerts on privileged actions that could weaken log integrity—such as deleting saved searches that power PCI dashboards or shrinking roles—supporting protection of audit trails from alteration.
implementation: (1) Restrict `_audit` visibility to security admin roles. (2) Forward `_audit` to immutable SIEM archive if required. (3) Map `user` to named individuals only. (4) Incident response playbook for suspected tampering.
visualization: Table (object, actors), timeline (first), single value (daily delete count).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
